_comment: News feeds power the main story feed. tool_feeds power the daily tool drop. hint = default category nudge given to the classifier. tier = 'journalism' (official blogs, professional newsrooms -- eligible for the Daily Blip). Reddit history, for anyone reading this later: removed 2026-07-11 (unauthenticated requests blocked from datacenter IPs), re-enabled 2026-07-13 with a real OAuth app + a two-stage quality gate (numeric thresholds + actual comment-sentiment reading), then removed AGAIN 2026-07-13 same day once Reddit's 'Responsible Builder Policy' turned out to require manual approval for new API access (no longer 2-minute self-service) -- owner decided the wait/rejection odds weren't worth it, and isn't a Reddit fan regardless. The OAuth-based fetcher, the two-stage gate, and the showcase_small tier all still exist in pipeline/lib/reddit.js, fully dormant -- reactivating just needs approved credentials and the source entries added back (see git history for the exact 5-subreddit block if wanted later). Add/remove sources freely — the pipeline adapts.

feeds.name: Runway Research
feeds.disabled_reason: No working RSS feed found anywhere on runwayml.com as of 2026 -- they publish to /news and /changelog pages with no <link rel=alternate> RSS pointer. Re-enable if you find a working feed URL.

feeds.name: Anthropic News
feeds.disabled_reason: Anthropic discontinued their public RSS feed entirely -- confirmed via independent re-verification in 2026 (their news page carries no RSS pointer anymore). Not a wrong URL issue; there's genuinely no feed to point at. Anthropic coverage still reaches the site via TechCrunch, The Verge, and OpenAI/industry coverage of Claude releases.

feeds.name: MarkTechPost
feeds.disabled_reason: Their feed has more than one kind of XML malformation. First fix (escaping bare ampersands, 2026-07-12) resolved one error but exposed a second, different one ('unexpected close tag') on the very next run. Rather than keep guessing at increasingly specific patches for genuinely broken XML on their end, disabled after two attempts. Re-enable if they ever fix their feed, or if you want to try again.

feeds.name: Cloudflare Blog
feeds._comment: Major industry outlet, per request. Note: Reuters no longer runs public RSS feeds (confirmed dead as of 2026) and Fortune's exact current feed URL couldn't be confidence-verified, so neither was added speculatively -- adding a guessed URL just to have it would likely show up as a permanent 'feed failed' entry. If you find working feed URLs for either, send them over and they're a one-line add to this file.

feeds.name: Artlist Blog
feeds.disabled_reason: Still 403s even after switching to a realistic browser User-Agent (tried 2026-07-12) -- their bot-protection is likely a JavaScript challenge that a simple server-side fetch can never pass, not a header-based filter. Fixing this properly would need a headless-browser fetch, which is out of scope for this pipeline's architecture. Disabled rather than left burning a failed request every 20 minutes.

feeds.name: VentureBeat AI
feeds._comment: Confirmed live 2026-07-12 via direct fetch -- high frequency, broad AI industry coverage.

feeds.name: DailyAI
feeds._comment: Confirmed live 2026-07-12 -- daily AI news publication, real RSS content-type verified.

feeds.name: Unite.AI
feeds._comment: Confirmed live 2026-07-12 -- covers AI tools/products frequently, real RSS content-type verified.

feeds.name: Creative Bloq AI
feeds._comment: Confirmed live 2026-07-12 via direct fetch (application/xml). Broad art/design/tech site with a dedicated AI section; classifier filters for creator relevance same as other broad-focus sources.

feeds.name: PetaPixel
feeds._comment: Confirmed live 2026-07-12 via direct fetch -- real current content, multiple posts/day. Mostly camera gear but regularly covers AI photo/video editing tools (e.g. Apple Image Playground) -- classifier filters for relevance.

feeds.name: Midjourney Updates
feeds._comment: Added 2026-07-13. Confirmed the base site (updates.midjourney.com) is a live, actively-posted Ghost blog (meta-generator: Ghost 6.51, posts multiple times/month, sometimes same-day) -- Ghost always exposes /rss/ as a platform default, though the exact XML wasn't independently fetched due to tool restrictions. If this shows 'feed failed' on the first run, tell me and I'll re-verify the exact path. Closer to source than waiting for journalism coverage of Midjourney releases.
